mappings.rationale: Directly covers the scientific study of language including phonetics, grammar, semantics, and linguistic theory.
Directly covers the diversity of languages worldwide, language classification, and language typology.
Directly covers language and society including dialects, language as a political instrument, and language in cross-cultural relations.
Covers a major Indo-European Germanic language, its history, dialects, and global spread.
Covers multilingual societies, pidgins and creoles, and the role of language in cross-cultural relations.
Covers language variants including slang and specialized variants like jargon, directly relevant to subsection C.3.
Covers cross-linguistic communication and the role of language in cross-cultural relations.
Directly covers written language systems including hieroglyphic, cuneiform, alphabetic, and ideographic writing.
Directly covers Egyptian hieroglyphic writing systems, relevant to subsection E.3.
Covers meaning and style in language, persuasive use of language, and language as a political instrument.
Directly covers cryptology including codes, ciphers, and other means of encrypting language (subsection C.8).
Covers computer languages, human language-computer interfaces, and AI, relevant to cybernetic communication (subsection B.4).
Covers psychological bases of speech, language processing, and the relationship between language and thought.

mappings.vsiAuthor: Emma Borg and Sarah A. Fisher
mappings.rationale: Directly covers semantics, meaning in language, and language and conceptualization (subsection C.5 and F.5).
Covers the interpretation of texts and the relationship between language and meaning.
Covers communication theory including signals, signs, symbols, and information systems relevant to subsection B.3.
Covers reading disorders relevant to speech disorders and the physiological bases of language (subsection D.3).
Covers written language, literacy, and the relationship between written and spoken language.
Covers computer languages and human-computer interfaces, relevant to cybernetic communication.
Illustrates a major Romance language literary tradition, relevant to the Indo-European language family.
Illustrates a major Germanic language literary tradition, relevant to the Indo-European language family.
Covers the literary tradition of the Chinese language, relevant to Sino-Tibetan languages and East Asian writing systems.
Covers the literary tradition of the Japanese language, relevant to the section on Japanese as a language.
Covers nonverbal communication through music, relevant to nonword sounds and non-linguistic communication.
Covers lexicography and the codification of language, relevant to linguistic change and the study of language.
Covers cross-linguistic literary study, relevant to language in cross-cultural relations and translation.
Covers the visual representation of written language, relevant to writing systems and adjuncts to writing.
Covers human-robot communication interfaces, relevant to cybernetic communication.
